name: Linked logo block
description: The linked logo block is a content unit that displays a logo with a link to additional pages or resources. This block is used as the primary content unit in the [Linked logo section](/sites/construct/linked-logo-section).
usage:
The Logo block is the primary content element of the [Linked logo section](/sites/construct/linked-logo-section). It can also be used as a block item within the  [Basic section](/sites/construct/basic-section) , [Divided section](/sites/construct/divided-section) , and [Tab section](/sites/construct/tab-section).

While the [Logo block](/sites/construct/logo-block) is a non-interactive visual listing of images, the Linked logo block introduces interaction by adding a [Link](/sites/construct/linked-logo-block#anatomy) below the [Logo container](/sites/construct/linked-logo-block#anatomy).

The block width follows the content container of its parent pattern, with options of 50, 75, and full-width.

### When to use

*   Each logo needs to link to additional pages or resources
*   Logos function as navigational elements

### When not to use

*   If logos are purely decorative or editorial and do not require interaction, use the [Logo block](/sites/construct/logo-block) instead

table.name: Logo container
table.description: The logo container wraps each logo image, uses a 16:9 aspect ratio, and includes an alt background

table.name: Rule
table.description: The muted rule appears below the logo container

table.name: Link
table.description: The link directs users to more detailed or related information

properties.name: Linked logo image
properties.description: 16:9 image container with the logo image inside

properties.name: Link text
properties.description: The link text for the logo
